name: API Gateway Pattern
strategicIntent: Provide a centralized, secure API gateway for all consumer-facing and partner APIs, enforcing consistent authentication, rate-limiting, routing, and API lifecycle management across McCain's digital ecosystem.
problemStatement: Without a centralized gateway, API consumers connect directly to backend services, leading to inconsistent authentication enforcement, no rate-limiting, difficulty in API versioning, and inability to monitor API usage patterns across the enterprise.

useCases.use: External partner APIs for order management and supply chain integration
useCases.dontUse: Internal service-to-service calls within a single Kubernetes cluster

useCases.use: Mobile and web BFF endpoints requiring OAuth2 token validation
useCases.dontUse: Batch file transfers or SFTP-based integrations

useCases.use: Public developer portal APIs with tiered rate-limiting
useCases.dontUse: Low-latency event streaming where gateway hop adds unacceptable latency

components: Azure API Management — gateway runtime, policy engine, and developer portal
Azure AD B2C — consumer and partner identity federation
Azure Key Vault — API signing keys and certificate storage
Azure Monitor & Application Insights — API analytics, latency tracking, and alerting
Azure Front Door — global load balancing and WAF for API endpoints
interactionSteps: API consumer authenticates via OAuth2 / Azure AD B2C and obtains JWT token
Request hits Azure Front Door for global routing and WAF inspection
API Management validates JWT, applies rate-limit and quota policies
Gateway routes request to backend service based on URL path and version
Response transformation policies normalize backend responses to API contract
Application Insights captures request telemetry and latency metrics
API Management publishes usage analytics to Azure Monitor dashboards
securityConsiderations: All API endpoints must require OAuth2 bearer token validation
Rate-limiting policies must be applied per subscription tier
Backend services must not be directly accessible — gateway is the sole ingress
API keys must be rotated every 90 days via Key Vault
WAF rules must be enabled on Azure Front Door for OWASP Top 10 protection
Mutual TLS required for partner-grade API integrations

guardrails.rule: All APIs must enforce OAuth2 authentication at the gateway
Rate-limiting must be configured per API product tier
All APIs must be registered in the API catalog before production deployment
APIs must support at least one prior version during deprecation window

exceptionProcess: Submit API Architecture Review request with justification for direct backend exposure. Requires Integration Architecture Board and CISO approval. Compensating controls must include WAF, IP whitelisting, and enhanced logging.

revisionHistory.author: Priya Sharma
revisionHistory.summary: Initial API gateway pattern with basic routing and auth

revisionHistory.author: Priya Sharma
revisionHistory.summary: Added rate-limiting tiers and developer portal integration

revisionHistory.author: Priya Sharma
revisionHistory.summary: Added Azure Front Door WAF, mutual TLS for partners, and API versioning policy
